children.label: A
children.text: an assault or other infliction or threat of infliction of death or bodily harm on an individual; or

children.label: B
children.text: damage to, or destruction of, real or personal property.

children.label: A
children.text: all articles enumerated in (ch. 465, ); andsection 14(a) of the Act of July 8, 193247 Stat. 654

children.label: B
children.text: a device designed to expel or hurl a projectile capable of causing injury to individuals or property, a dagger, a dirk, a stiletto, and a knife having a blade over three inches in length.

children.heading: .—Explosives
children.label: 3 .—Explosives
children.text: The term “explosives” has the meaning given that term in .section 841(d) of title 18

children.heading: .—Firearm
children.label: 4 .—Firearm
children.text: The term “firearm” has the meaning given that term in section 921(3) of title 18.1So in original. Probably should be “921(a)(3)”.1

children.heading: .—Obstruction of Roads
children.label: b .—Obstruction of Roads
children.text: A person may not occupy the roads in the United States Capitol Grounds in a manner that obstructs or hinders their proper use, or use the roads in the area of the Grounds, south of Constitution Avenue and B Street and north of Independence Avenue and B Street, to convey goods or merchandise, except to or from the United States Capitol on Federal Government service.

children.heading: .—Sale of Articles, Display of Signs, and Solicitations
children.label: c .—Sale of Articles, Display of Signs, and Solicitations

children.text: offer or expose any article for sale.
display a sign, placard, or other form of advertisement.
solicit fares, alms, subscriptions, or contributions.

children.heading: .—Injuries to Property
children.label: d .—Injuries to Property
children.text: A person may not step or climb on, remove, or in any way injure any statue, seat, wall, fountain, or other erection or architectural feature, or any tree, shrub, plant, or turf, in the Grounds.

children.heading: Capitol Grounds and Buildings Security.—
children.label: e Capitol Grounds and Buildings Security.—

children.heading: .—Firearms, dangerous weapons, explosives, or incendiary devices
children.label: 1 .—Firearms, dangerous weapons, explosives, or incendiary devices
i
children.text: may not carry on or have readily accessible to any individual on the Grounds or in any of the Capitol Buildings a firearm, a dangerous weapon, explosives, or an incendiary device;

children.label: ii
children.text: may not discharge a firearm or explosives, use a dangerous weapon, or ignite an incendiary device, on the Grounds or in any of the Capitol Buildings; or

children.label: iii
children.text: may not transport on the Grounds or in any of the Capitol Buildings explosives or an incendiary device; or

children.label: B
children.text: may not knowingly, with force and violence, enter or remain on the floor of either House of Congress.

children.label: A
children.text: enter or remain on the floor of either House of Congress or in any cloakroom or lobby adjacent to that floor, in the Rayburn Room of the House of Representatives, or in the Marble Room of the Senate, unless authorized to do so pursuant to rules adopted, or an authorization given, by that House;

children.label: B
children.text: enter or remain in the gallery of either House of Congress in violation of rules governing admission to the gallery adopted by that House or pursuant to an authorization given by that House;

children.label: i
children.text: either House of Congress or a Member, committee, officer, or employee of Congress, or either House of Congress; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Library of Congress;

children.label: D
children.text: utter loud, threatening, or abusive language, or engage in disorderly or disruptive conduct, at any place in the Grounds or in any of the Capitol Buildings with the intent to impede, disrupt, or disturb the orderly conduct of a session of Congress or either House of Congress, or the orderly conduct in that building of a hearing before, or any deliberations of, a committee of Congress or either House of Congress;

children.label: E
children.text: obstruct, or impede passage through or within, the Grounds or any of the Capitol Buildings;

children.label: F
children.text: engage in an act of physical violence in the Grounds or any of the Capitol Buildings; or

children.label: G
children.text: parade, demonstrate, or picket in any of the Capitol Buildings.

children.label: A
children.text: a Member of Congress;

children.label: B
children.text: an employee of a Member of Congress;

children.label: C
children.text: an officer or employee of Congress or a committee of Congress; or

children.label: D
children.text: an officer or employee of either House of Congress or a committee of that House.

children.heading: .—Parades, Assemblages, and Display of Flags
children.label: f .—Parades, Assemblages, and Display of Flags

children.text: parade, stand, or move in processions or assemblages in the Grounds; or
display in the Grounds a flag, banner, or device designed or adapted to bring into public notice a party, organization, or movement.
